title: SSC CGL T-I Quant Section Test

questions.question: The ratio of the ages of Meena and Meera is 4:3 and sum of their ages is 28 years. The ratio of their ages after 8 years will be:
questions.explanation: ‚ÄãGiven: Ratio of ages (Meena:Meera)=4:3 Sum of ages=28 years Time elapsed=8 years Solution: Let the current ages be 4x and 3x. 4x+3x=28 7x=28 x=4 Current ages: Meena=4√ó4=16 years Meera=3√ó4=12 years Ages after 8 years: Meena=16+8=24 Meera=12+8=20 New Ratio=24:20 Simplify by dividing by 4: 6:5 Final Answer 6:5\textbf{Given:} \\ \space \, \\ \text{Ratio of ages (Meena:Meera)} = 4:3 \\ \space \, \\ \text{Sum of ages} = 28 \text{ years} \\ \space \, \\ \text{Time elapsed} = 8 \text{ years} \\ \space \, \\ \textbf{Solution:} \\ \space \, \\ \text{Let the current ages be } 4x \text{ and } 3x. \\ \space \, \\ 4x + 3x = 28 \\ \space \, \\ 7x = 28 \\ \space \, \\ x = 4 \\ \space \, \\ \text{Current ages:} \\ \space \, \\ \text{Meena} = 4 \times 4 = 16 \text{ years} \\ \space \, \\ \text{Meera} = 3 \times 4 = 12 \text{ years} \\ \space \, \\ \text{Ages after 8 years:} \\ \space \, \\ \text{Meena} = 16 + 8 = 24 \\ \space \, \\ \text{Meera} = 12 + 8 = 20 \\ \space \, \\ \text{New Ratio} = 24 : 20 \\ \space \, \\ \text{Simplify by dividing by 4:} \\ \space \, \\ 6 : 5 \\ \space \, \\ \textbf{Final Answer} \\ \space \, \\ 6:5Given: Ratio of ages (Meena:Meera)=4:3 Sum of ages=28 years Time elapsed=8 years Solution: Let the current ages be 4x and 3x. 4x+3x=28 7x=28 x=4 Current ages: Meena=4√ó4=16 years Meera=3√ó4=12 years Ages after 8 years: Meena=16+8=24 Meera=12+8=20 New Ratio=24:20 Simplify by dividing by 4: 6:5 Final Answer 6:5‚Äã‚Äã

questions.question: Two trains, A and B, start simultaneously from stations X and Y towards each other. The distance between X and Y is 540 km. After crossing each other, train A takes 4 hours to reach Y and train B takes 9 hours to reach X. Find the speed of the faster train (in km/h).
questions.explanation: Given: Distance = 540 km. Time taken after meeting: tA=4hrs,tB=9t_A = 4 hrs, t_B = 9tA‚Äã=4hrs,tB‚Äã=9‚Äã hrs. Formula Used: 1. Ratio of speeds: SASB=tBtA\frac{S_A}{S_B} = \sqrt{\frac{t_B}{t_A}}\\SB‚ÄãSA‚Äã‚Äã=tA‚ÄãtB‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã 2. Total Distance =(SA+SB)√ótime to meet= (S_A + S_B) \times \text{time to meet}\\=(SA‚Äã+SB‚Äã)√ótime to meet‚Äã‚Äã 3. Time to meet t Solution: Calculate speed ratio. ‚ÄãSASB=94=32\frac{S_A}{S_B} = \sqrt{\frac{9}{4}} = \frac{3}{2}\\SB‚ÄãSA‚Äã‚Äã=49‚Äã‚Äã=23‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã Let SA=3xS_A = 3xSA‚Äã=3x‚Äã and SB=2x.S_B = 2x.SB‚Äã=2x.‚Äã‚Äã Calculate time to meet. ‚Äãt=4√ó9=6hours.t = \sqrt{4 \times 9} = 6 hours.t=4√ó9‚Äã=6hours.‚Äã‚Äã Use distance to find x. Distance =(SA+SB)√ót = (S_A + S_B) \times t\\=(SA‚Äã+SB‚Äã)√ót‚Äã‚Äã ‚Äã540=(3x+2x)√ó6540=30xx=18540 = (3x + 2x) \times 6\\540 = 30x\\x = 18\\540=(3x+2x)√ó6540=30xx=18‚Äã‚Äã Find faster speed. ‚ÄãSA=3(18)=54 km/hSB=2(18)=36 km/hS_A = 3(18) = 54 \text{ km/h}\\S_B = 2(18) = 36 \text{ km/h}\\SA‚Äã=3(18)=54 km/hSB‚Äã=2(18)=36 km/h‚Äã‚Äã Faster is 54 km/h. Exam-Hall Method: Ratio 3:2. Time meet 6h. Speed sum = 540/6 = 90. 3 units + 2 units = 90 ‚üπ \implies‚üπ‚Äã 1 unit = 18. Faster = 3 units = 54. So the correct answer is (b)

questions.question: The value of an article was ‚Çπ75. First its value was increased by 20% and then the increased value was decreased by 20%. What is the present value?
questions.explanation: ‚ÄãGiven: Initial Value=‚Çπ75 First change: Increase by 20% Second change: Decrease by 20% Formula Used: Final Value=Initial Value√ó(1+R1100)√ó(1‚àíR2100) Solution: Step 1: Increase by 20% New Value=75√ó(1+20100) =75√ó1.2=90 Step 2: Decrease by 20% Final Value=90√ó(1‚àí20100) =90√ó0.8 =72 Final Answer ‚Çπ72\textbf{Given:} \\ \space \, \\ \text{Initial Value} = ‚Çπ75 \\ \space \, \\ \text{First change: Increase by } 20\% \\ \space \, \\ \text{Second change: Decrease by } 20\% \\ \space \, \\ \textbf{Formula Used:} \\ \space \, \\ \text{Final Value} = \text{Initial Value} \times \left(1 + \frac{R_1}{100}\right) \times \left(1 - \frac{R_2}{100}\right) \\ \space \, \\ \textbf{Solution:} \\ \space \, \\ \text{Step 1: Increase by 20\%} \\ \space \, \\ \text{New Value} = 75 \times \left(1 + \frac{20}{100}\right) \\ \space \, \\ = 75 \times 1.2 = 90 \\ \space \, \\ \text{Step 2: Decrease by 20\%} \\ \space \, \\ \text{Final Value} = 90 \times \left(1 - \frac{20}{100}\right) \\ \space \, \\ = 90 \times 0.8 \\ \space \, \\ = 72 \\ \space \, \\ \textbf{Final Answer} \\ \space \, \\ ‚Çπ72Given: Initial Value=‚Çπ75 First change: Increase by 20% Second change: Decrease by 20% Formula Used: Final Value=Initial Value√ó(1+100R1‚Äã‚Äã)√ó(1‚àí100R2‚Äã‚Äã) Solution: Step 1: Increase by 20% New Value=75√ó(1+10020‚Äã) =75√ó1.2=90 Step 2: Decrease by 20% Final Value=90√ó(1‚àí10020‚Äã) =90√ó0.8 =72 Final Answer ‚Çπ72‚Äã‚Äã

questions.question: If 3 workers and 5 assistants can complete a work in 10 days, while 18 workers and 35 assistants can complete the same in 1.5 days, how much time(approximate) will 10 workers and 12 assistants take to complete the same work?
questions.explanation: ‚ÄãGiven: (3W+5A)√ó10=(18W+35A)√ó1.5 Solution: Equating total work: 30W+50A=27W+52.5A 3W=2.5A=&gtWA=56 Let efficiency of W = 5 units/day, A = 6 units/day. Total Work=10(3(5)+5(6))=10(15+30)=450 units. New Team Efficiency=10(5)+12(6)=50+72=122 units/day. Time taken=450122=22561‚âà3.688 days. 0.688 days‚âà16.5 hours. Total time‚âà3 days 16.5 hours. Final Answer Approx 3 days 17 hours \textbf{Given:} \\ \space \, \\ (3W + 5A) \times 10 = (18W + 35A) \times 1.5 \\ \space \, \\ \textbf{Solution:} \\ \space \, \\ \text{Equating total work:} \\ \space \, \\ 30W + 50A = 27W + 52.5A \\ \space \, \\ 3W = 2.5A \Rightarrow \frac{W}{A} = \frac{5}{6} \\ \space \, \\ \text{Let efficiency of W = 5 units/day, A = 6 units/day.} \\ \space \, \\ \text{Total Work} = 10(3(5) + 5(6)) = 10(15 + 30) = 450 \text{ units.} \\ \space \, \\ \text{New Team Efficiency} = 10(5) + 12(6) = 50 + 72 = 122 \text{ units/day.} \\ \space \, \\ \text{Time taken} = \frac{450}{122} = \frac{225}{61} \approx 3.688 \text{ days.} \\ \space \, \\ 0.688 \text{ days} \approx 16.5 \text{ hours.} \\ \space \, \\ \text{Total time} \approx 3 \text{ days } 16.5 \text{ hours.} \\ \space \, \\ \textbf{Final Answer} \\ \space \, \\ \text{Approx } 3 \text{ days } 17 \text{ hours }Given: (3W+5A)√ó10=(18W+35A)√ó1.5 Solution: Equating total work: 30W+50A=27W+52.5A 3W=2.5A=&gtAW‚Äã=65‚Äã Let efficiency of W = 5 units/day, A = 6 units/day. Total Work=10(3(5)+5(6))=10(15+30)=450 units. New Team Efficiency=10(5)+12(6)=50+72=122 units/day. Time taken=122450‚Äã=61225‚Äã‚âà3.688 days. 0.688 days‚âà16.5 hours. Total time‚âà3 days 16.5 hours. Final Answer Approx 3 days 17 hours ‚Äã‚Äã

questions.question: If 3 √ó sin Œ∏ √ó cos(90¬∞ - Œ∏) = 1, where 0¬∞ < Œ∏ < 90¬∞, then find the value of sin(2Œ∏) + cos(2Œ∏).
questions.explanation: ‚ÄãGiven: 3sin‚Å°Œ∏cos‚Å°(90‚àò‚àíŒ∏)=1 Formula Used: cos‚Å°(90‚àò‚àíŒ∏)=sin‚Å°Œ∏ sin‚Å°2Œ∏=2sin‚Å°Œ∏cos‚Å°Œ∏,cos‚Å°2Œ∏=1‚àí2sin‚Å°2Œ∏ Solution: Simplify the given equation: 3sin‚Å°Œ∏(sin‚Å°Œ∏)=1 sin‚Å°2Œ∏=13=&gtsin‚Å°Œ∏=13 Calculate cos‚Å°Œ∏: cos‚Å°Œ∏=1‚àísin‚Å°2Œ∏=1‚àí13=23 Calculate sin‚Å°2Œ∏ and cos‚Å°2Œ∏: sin‚Å°2Œ∏=2(13)(23)=223 cos‚Å°2Œ∏=1‚àí2(13)=13 Sum: sin‚Å°2Œ∏+cos‚Å°2Œ∏=223+13=22+13 Final Answer 22+13\textbf{Given:} \\ \space \, \\ 3 \sin \theta \cos(90^\circ - \theta) = 1 \\ \space \, \\ \textbf{Formula Used:} \\ \space \, \\ \cos(90^\circ - \theta) = \sin \theta \\ \space \, \\ \sin 2\theta = 2\sin\theta\cos\theta, \quad \cos 2\theta = 1 - 2\sin^2\theta \\ \space \, \\ \textbf{Solution:} \\ \space \, \\ \text{Simplify the given equation:} \\ \space \, \\ 3 \sin \theta (\sin \theta) = 1 \\ \space \, \\ \sin^2 \theta = \frac{1}{3} \Rightarrow \sin \theta = \frac{1}{\sqrt{3}} \\ \space \, \\ \text{Calculate } \cos \theta: \\ \space \, \\ \cos \theta = \sqrt{1 - \sin^2 \theta} = \sqrt{1 - \frac{1}{3}} = \sqrt{\frac{2}{3}} \\ \space \, \\ \text{Calculate } \sin 2\theta \text{ and } \cos 2\theta: \\ \space \, \\ \sin 2\theta = 2 \left(\frac{1}{\sqrt{3}}\right) \left(\frac{\sqrt{2}}{\sqrt{3}}\right) = \frac{2\sqrt{2}}{3} \\ \space \, \\ \cos 2\theta = 1 - 2\left(\frac{1}{3}\right) = \frac{1}{3} \\ \space \, \\ \text{Sum:} \\ \space \, \\ \sin 2\theta + \cos 2\theta = \frac{2\sqrt{2}}{3} + \frac{1}{3} = \frac{2\sqrt{2} + 1}{3} \\ \space \, \\ \textbf{Final Answer} \\ \space \, \\ \frac{2\sqrt{2} + 1}{3}Given: 3sinŒ∏cos(90‚àò‚àíŒ∏)=1 Formula Used: cos(90‚àò‚àíŒ∏)=sinŒ∏ sin2Œ∏=2sinŒ∏cosŒ∏,cos2Œ∏=1‚àí2sin2Œ∏ Solution: Simplify the given equation: 3sinŒ∏(sinŒ∏)=1 sin2Œ∏=31‚Äã=&gtsinŒ∏=3‚Äã1‚Äã Calculate cosŒ∏: cosŒ∏=1‚àísin2Œ∏‚Äã=1‚àí31‚Äã‚Äã=32‚Äã‚Äã Calculate sin2Œ∏ and cos2Œ∏: sin2Œ∏=2(3‚Äã1‚Äã)(3‚Äã2‚Äã‚Äã)=322‚Äã‚Äã cos2Œ∏=1‚àí2(31‚Äã)=31‚Äã Sum: sin2Œ∏+cos2Œ∏=322‚Äã‚Äã+31‚Äã=322‚Äã+1‚Äã Final Answer 322‚Äã+1‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã

questions.question: Which of the following are divisible by 2, 5 and 9? A. 4680 B. 7245 C. 8190 D. 3420 Choose the correct option :
questions.explanation: Concept Used: 1. Divisibility by 2: Last digit is even. 2. Divisibility by 5: Last digit is 0 or 5. 3. Divisibility by 2 and 5 (i.e., 10): Last digit must be 0. 4. Divisibility by 9: Sum of digits is divisible by 9. Solution: Check condition for 2 and 5 (Must end in 0). A. 4680 (Ends in 0) - Pass B. 7245 (Ends in 5) - Fail (Not divisible by 2) C. 8190 (Ends in 0) - Pass D. 3420 (Ends in 0) - Pass Check divisibility by 9 for passing numbers. A. 4680: 4+6+8+0 = 18 (Divisible by 9) - Yes C. 8190: 8+1+9+0 = 18 (Divisible by 9) - Yes D. 3420: 3+4+2+0 = 9 (Divisible by 9) - Yes Conclusion. Numbers A, C, and D satisfy all conditions. So the correct answer is (b)

questions.question: The sum of 128+136+145+155+....14950\frac{1}{28}+\frac{1}{36}+\frac{1}{45}+\frac{1}{55}+....\frac{1}{4950}281‚Äã+361‚Äã+451‚Äã+551‚Äã+....49501‚Äã‚Äã is:
questions.explanation: ‚ÄãGiven:128+136+145+155+‚ãØ+14950Observation:128=27‚ãÖ8,136=28‚ãÖ9,145=29‚ãÖ10, ‚Ä¶, 14950=299‚ãÖ100=&gtGeneral term =1n(n+1)Formula used:1n(n+1)=1n‚àí1n+1Solution:S=2[(17‚àí18)+(18‚àí19)+(19‚àí110)+‚ãØ+(199‚àí1100)]S=2[17‚àí1100]S=2[100‚àí7700]S=93350Correct option: (A)\textbf{Given:}\quad\frac{1}{28} + \frac{1}{36} + \frac{1}{45} + \frac{1}{55} + \cdots + \frac{1}{4950}\\[8pt]\textbf{Observation:}\\[4pt]\frac{1}{28} = \frac{2}{7 \cdot 8}, \quad\frac{1}{36} = \frac{2}{8 \cdot 9}, \quad\frac{1}{45} = \frac{2}{9 \cdot 10}, \ \ldots ,\ \frac{1}{4950} = \frac{2}{99 \cdot 100}\\[8pt]\Rightarrow \text{General term } = \frac{1}{n(n+1)}\\[10pt]\textbf{Formula used:}\\[4pt]\frac{1}{n(n+1)} = \frac{1}{n} - \frac{1}{n+1}\\[12pt]\textbf{Solution:}\\[6pt]S =2\left [\left(\frac{1}{7} - \frac{1}{8}\right)+ \left(\frac{1}{8} - \frac{1}{9}\right)+ \left(\frac{1}{9} - \frac{1}{10}\right)+ \cdots+ \left(\frac{1}{99} - \frac{1}{100}\right)\right]\\[10pt]S = 2[ \frac{1}{7} - \frac{1}{100}]\\[8pt]S = 2[\frac{100 - 7}{700}]\\[8pt]S = \frac{93}{350}\\[10pt]\textbf{Correct option: (A)}Given:281‚Äã+361‚Äã+451‚Äã+551‚Äã+‚ãØ+49501‚ÄãObservation:281‚Äã=7‚ãÖ82‚Äã,361‚Äã=8‚ãÖ92‚Äã,451‚Äã=9‚ãÖ102‚Äã, ‚Ä¶, 49501‚Äã=99‚ãÖ1002‚Äã=&gtGeneral term =n(n+1)1‚ÄãFormula used:n(n+1)1‚Äã=n1‚Äã‚àín+11‚ÄãSolution:S=2[(71‚Äã‚àí81‚Äã)+(81‚Äã‚àí91‚Äã)+(91‚Äã‚àí101‚Äã)+‚ãØ+(991‚Äã‚àí1001‚Äã)]S=2[71‚Äã‚àí1001‚Äã]S=2[700100‚àí7‚Äã]S=35093‚ÄãCorrect option: (A)‚Äã‚Äã

questions.question: A sum of money becomes 4 times itself in 15 years at simple interest. In how many years will it become 7 times itself at the same rate?
questions.explanation: ‚ÄãGiven:A=4P,T=15 yearsConcept Used:Simple Interest is directly proportional to timeFormula Used:A=P(1+RT100)Solution:4P=P(1+15R100)4=1+15R10015R100=3R=207P=P(1+20T100)7=1+20T10020T100=6T=30Final Answer:30 years\textbf{Given:}\\A = 4P,\quad T = 15\ \text{years}\\[6pt]\textbf{Concept Used:}\\\text{Simple Interest is directly proportional to time}\\[6pt]\textbf{Formula Used:}\\A = P\left(1 + \frac{RT}{100}\right)\\[6pt]\textbf{Solution:}\\4P = P\left(1 + \frac{15R}{100}\right)\\4 = 1 + \frac{15R}{100}\\\frac{15R}{100} = 3\\R = 20\\[6pt]7P = P\left(1 + \frac{20T}{100}\right)\\7 = 1 + \frac{20T}{100}\\\frac{20T}{100} = 6\\T = 30\\[6pt]\textbf{Final Answer:}\\\boxed{30\ \text{years}}Given:A=4P,T=15 yearsConcept Used:Simple Interest is directly proportional to timeFormula Used:A=P(1+100RT‚Äã)Solution:4P=P(1+10015R‚Äã)4=1+10015R‚Äã10015R‚Äã=3R=207P=P(1+10020T‚Äã)7=1+10020T‚Äã10020T‚Äã=6T=30Final Answer:30 years‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã

questions.question: What is the mean of the mode, median, and range of the data given below? 24, 15, 18, 20, 16, 21, 17, 18, 23, 30, 22, 14
questions.explanation: Given data 24, 15, 18, 20, 16, 21, 17, 18, 23, 30, 22, 14 Arrange data in ascending order 14, 15, 16, 17, 18, 18, 20, 21, 22, 23, 24, 30 Find Mode The number that occurs most often is 18 Mode = 18 Find Median There are 12 values, so median is the average of the 6th and 7th terms. 6th term = 18 7th term = 20 Median = (18 + 20) / 2 = 19 Find Range Range = Highest value ‚àí Lowest value Range = 30 ‚àí 14 = 16 Mean of Mode, Median, and Range Mean = (18 + 19 + 16) / 3 Mean = 53 / 3 Mean ‚âà 17.7

questions.question: A and B can complete a piece of work in 10 days and 12 days, respectively. If they work on alternate days beginning with A, then in how many days will the work be completed?
questions.explanation: Given: A can complete the work in 10 days. B can complete the work in 12 days. They work on alternate days, starting with A. Solution: A's work rate 110\frac{1}{10}101‚Äã of the work per day. ‚ÄãB's work rate 112\frac{1}{12}121‚Äã of the work per day. Working on Alternate Days: Work done by A in 1 day: 110\frac{1}{10}101‚Äã ‚ÄãWork done by B in 1 day: 112\frac{1}{12}121‚Äã ‚Äã‚ÄãWork done in 2 days 110\frac{1}{10}101‚Äã + 112\frac{1}{12}121‚Äã = 1160\frac{11}{60}6011‚Äã ‚Äã‚ÄãIn every 2 days, they complete 1160\frac{11}{60}6011‚Äã of the work 2 - day cycles are needed to complete the entire work: After 5 cycles (10 days): Total work done = 5 √ó\times√ó 1160\frac{11}{60}6011‚Äã = 5560\frac{55}{60}6055‚Äã = 1112\frac{11}{12}1211‚Äã ‚Äã‚ÄãRemaining work after 10 days: Remaining work = 1- 1112\frac{11}{12}1211‚Äã = 112\frac{1}{12}121‚Äã ‚Äã‚ÄãOn the 11th day, A will work- A's work rate for 1 day = 110\frac{1}{10}101‚Äã ‚Äã‚ÄãSince the remaining work 112\frac{1}{12}121‚Äã is less than 110\frac{1}{10}101‚Äã, A will complete the task on the 11th day. ‚ÄãTime taken by A to complete 112\frac{1}{12}121‚Äã of the work Time = 112110\frac{\frac{1}{12}}{\frac{1}{10}}101‚Äã121‚Äã‚Äã = 1012\frac{10}{12}1210‚Äã = 56\frac{5}{6}65‚Äã day ‚ÄãThe work will be completed in 10 56\frac{5}{6}65‚Äã days ‚Äã

questions.question: If F, V and E are respectively the number of faces, vertices and edges of a pentagonal prism, then which of the following statements is true?
questions.explanation: Given: Solid: Pentagonal Prism. Concept Used: For a prism with base sides n: Faces F = n + 2 Vertices V = 2n Edges E = 3n Solution: Step 1: Determine F, V, E for Pentagonal Prism (n=5). F = 5 + 2 = 7 V = 2(5) = 10 E = 3(5) = 15 Check Option A. 2F + 3V - 2E = 2(7) + 3(10) - 2(15) = 14 + 30 - 30 = 14 (True) So the correct answer is (a)

questions.question: By which number should (‚àí23)‚àí1\left(-\frac{2}{3}\right)^{-1}(‚àí32‚Äã)‚àí1‚Äã be multiplied so that the product is the reciprocal of (‚àí53)‚àí1\left(-\frac{5}{3}\right)^{-1}(‚àí35‚Äã)‚àí1‚Äã?
questions.explanation: Given: Number 1:(‚àí23)‚àí1=‚àí32 \left(-\frac{2}{3}\right)^{-1} = -\frac{3}{2}\\(‚àí32‚Äã)‚àí1=‚àí23‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã Target Product: Reciprocal of (‚àí53)‚àí1 \left(-\frac{5}{3}\right)^{-1}\\(‚àí35‚Äã)‚àí1‚Äã‚Äã Solution: Simplify the target. ‚Äã(‚àí53)‚àí1=‚àí35\left(-\frac{5}{3}\right)^{-1} = -\frac{3}{5}\\(‚àí35‚Äã)‚àí1=‚àí53‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã Reciprocal of this is ‚àí53.-\frac{5}{3}.‚àí35‚Äã.‚Äã‚Äã Set up the equation. Let the number be x. ‚Äãx√ó(‚àí32)=‚àí53x=(‚àí53)√∑(‚àí32)x=(‚àí53)√ó(‚àí23)x=109x \times \left(-\frac{3}{2}\right) = -\frac{5}{3}\\x = \left(-\frac{5}{3}\right) \div \left(-\frac{3}{2}\right)\\x = \left(-\frac{5}{3}\right) \times \left(-\frac{2}{3}\right)\\x = \frac{10}{9}\\x√ó(‚àí23‚Äã)=‚àí35‚Äãx=(‚àí35‚Äã)√∑(‚àí23‚Äã)x=(‚àí35‚Äã)√ó(‚àí32‚Äã)x=910‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã So the correct answer is (c)

questions.question: Consider the following information and answer the questions based on it. 150 people from a locality commute daily for work. Out of them 25 travel only by bus, 40 only by train, 20 take both taxi and train, 30 use only private cars, 25 take bus and taxi and the remaining travel only by taxi. What is the percentage of people who use only one mode of transport?
questions.explanation: Given: Total people = 150 Only Bus = 25 Only Train = 40 Only Private Cars = 30 Solution: Total Taxi Users = (Only Taxi) + (Bus and Taxi) + (Taxi and Train) 25 + 40 + 20 + 30 + 25 = 140 Only Taxi = 150 ‚Äì 140 = 10 People using only one mode = 25(Bus) + 40(Train) + 30(Private Cars) + 10(Taxi) = 105 Percentage = (105150)√ó100=70%\left( \frac{105}{150} \right) \times 100 = 70\%(150105‚Äã)√ó100=70%‚Äã

questions.question: A ladder of length 50 cm is at an inclination of 45¬∞ to a wall with its bottom touching the ground. Find the length from the ground to the point where the ladder touches the wall.
questions.explanation: ‚ÄãGiven: Length of ladder (hypotenuse)=50 cm Angle with wall=45‚àò Concept Used: In a right-angled triangle formed by the ladder, wall, and ground: cos‚Å°Œ∏=Adwacent (Height)Hypotenuse Solution:\textbf{Given:} \\ \space \, \\ \text{Length of ladder (hypotenuse)} = 50 \text{ cm} \\ \space \, \\ \text{Angle with wall} = 45^\circ \\ \space \, \\ \textbf{Concept Used:} \\ \space \, \\ \text{In a right-angled triangle formed by the ladder, wall, and ground:} \\ \space \, \\ \cos \theta = \frac{\text{Adwacent (Height)}}{\text{Hypotenuse}} \\ \space \, \\ \textbf{Solution:}Given: Length of ladder (hypotenuse)=50 cm Angle with wall=45‚àò Concept Used: In a right-angled triangle formed by the ladder, wall, and ground: cosŒ∏=HypotenuseAdwacent (Height)‚Äã Solution:‚Äã‚Äã ![](https://cdn010.adda247.com/1ac720a5-2164-45a8-8ece-63abd1611130.png) ‚ÄãLet height be h. cos‚Å°45‚àò=h50 12=h50 h=502=252 cm Final Answer 252 cm \text{Let height be } h. \\ \space \, \\ \cos 45^\circ = \frac{h}{50} \\ \space \, \\ \frac{1}{\sqrt{2}} = \frac{h}{50} \\ \space \, \\ h = \frac{50}{\sqrt{2}} = 25\sqrt{2} \text{ cm} \\ \space \, \\ \textbf{Final Answer} \\ \space \, \\ 25\sqrt{2} \text{ cm}Let height be h. cos45‚àò=50h‚Äã 2‚Äã1‚Äã=50h‚Äã h=2‚Äã50‚Äã=252‚Äã cm Final Answer 252‚Äã cm‚Äã‚Äã

questions.question: If 141120=p2√óq6√ór1√ós2141120 = p^2 \times q^6 \times r^1 \times s^2141120=p2√óq6√ór1√ós2‚Äã, where p, q, rand sare prime numbers, then what is the value of (p + q + r - s)?
questions.explanation: Given ‚Äã141120=p2√óq6√ór1√ós2141120 = p^2 \times q^6 \times r^1 \times s^2141120=p2√óq6√ór1√ós2‚Äã‚Äã where p, q, r, s are prime numbers. Find the value of (p + q + r ‚àí s). Solution First, find the prime factorization of 141120. 141120 = 14112 √ó 10 = (14112) √ó (2 √ó 5) Now factorize 14112: 14112 = 2‚Åµ √ó 3¬≤ √ó 7¬≤ So, 141120 = 2‚Å∂ √ó 3¬≤ √ó 5¬π √ó 7¬≤ Compare with given form p¬≤ √ó q‚Å∂ √ó r¬π √ó s¬≤ Matching powers: q‚Å∂ = 2‚Å∂ => q = 2 p¬≤ = 3¬≤ => p = 3 r¬π = 5¬π => r = 5 s¬≤ = 7¬≤ => s = 7 Required value p + q + r ‚àí s = 3 + 2 + 5 ‚àí 7 = 3 Correct option: C

questions.question: The solution of the equation (x+2)‚àí(3x+5)2‚àí3x=111\frac{(x + 2) - (3x + 5)}{2 - 3x} = \frac{1}{11}2‚àí3x(x+2)‚àí(3x+5)‚Äã=111‚Äã‚Äã is also the solution of the equation :
questions.explanation: Given: Equation:(x+2)‚àí(3x+5)2‚àí3x=111 \frac{(x + 2) - (3x + 5)}{2 - 3x} = \frac{1}{11}\\2‚àí3x(x+2)‚àí(3x+5)‚Äã=111‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã Solution: Simplify the numerator. (x + 2) - (3x + 5) = x + 2 - 3x - 5 = -2x - 3 Solve for x. ‚Äã‚àí2x‚àí32‚àí3x=111\frac{-2x - 3}{2 - 3x} = \frac{1}{11}\\2‚àí3x‚àí2x‚àí3‚Äã=111‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã Cross-multiply: ‚Äã11(‚àí2x‚àí3)=1(2‚àí3x)‚àí22x‚àí33=2‚àí3x‚àí33‚àí2=‚àí3x+22x‚àí35=19x ‚üπ x=‚àí351911(-2x - 3) = 1(2 - 3x)\\-22x - 33 = 2 - 3x\\-33 - 2 = -3x + 22x\\-35 = 19x \implies x = -\frac{35}{19}\\11(‚àí2x‚àí3)=1(2‚àí3x)‚àí22x‚àí33=2‚àí3x‚àí33‚àí2=‚àí3x+22x‚àí35=19x‚üπx=‚àí1935‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã Option C: 1 ‚àí x = 18x + 36 Solve it: 1 ‚àí 36 = 18x + x ‚àí35 = 19x x = ‚àí35/19 So the correct answer is (c)

questions.question: The distance between the points (2, 3) and (4, 1) is.
questions.explanation: Given: Points: (2, 3) and (4, 1) Concept used: Distance between two points: ‚Äãd=(x2‚àíx1)2+(y2‚àíy1)2d = \sqrt{(x_2 - x_1)^2 + (y_2 - y_1)^2}d=(x2‚Äã‚àíx1‚Äã)2+(y2‚Äã‚àíy1‚Äã)2‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã Solution: ‚Äãd=(4‚àí2)2+(1‚àí3)2=(2)2+(‚àí2)2=4+4=8=22d = \sqrt{(4 - 2)^2 + (1 - 3)^2} \\= \sqrt{(2)^2 + (-2)^2} \\= \sqrt{4 + 4} \\= \sqrt{8} \\= 2\sqrt{2}d=(4‚àí2)2+(1‚àí3)2‚Äã=(2)2+(‚àí2)2‚Äã=4+4‚Äã=8‚Äã=22‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã Correct Answer is (c) 22\ 2\sqrt{2} 22‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã

questions.question: What is the lateral surface area of a pyramid whose base dimensions are 10 m √ó 6 m and the slant height is 15 m?
questions.explanation: ‚ÄãGiven:Base dimensions of the pyramid =10 m √ó6 mSlant height =15 mFormula Used:Lateral Surface Area of a pyramid=12√ó(Perimeter of base)√ó(Slant height)Solution:Perimeter of the rectangular base=2(10+6)=32 mLateral surface area=12√ó32√ó15=16√ó15=240 m2Correct Answer: 240 m2\textbf{Given:} \\\text{Base dimensions of the pyramid } = 10 \text{ m } \times 6 \text{ m} \\\text{Slant height } = 15 \text{ m}\\[6pt]\textbf{Formula Used:} \\\text{Lateral Surface Area of a pyramid} \\= \frac{1}{2} \times (\text{Perimeter of base}) \times (\text{Slant height})\\[8pt]\textbf{Solution:} \\\text{Perimeter of the rectangular base} \\= 2(10 + 6) = 32 \text{ m}\\[8pt]\text{Lateral surface area} \\= \frac{1}{2} \times 32 \times 15\\[6pt]= 16 \times 15\\[6pt]= 240 \text{ m}^2\\[8pt]\textbf{Correct Answer: } 240 \text{ m}^2Given:Base dimensions of the pyramid =10 m √ó6 mSlant height =15 mFormula Used:Lateral Surface Area of a pyramid=21‚Äã√ó(Perimeter of base)√ó(Slant height)Solution:Perimeter of the rectangular base=2(10+6)=32 mLateral surface area=21‚Äã√ó32√ó15=16√ó15=240 m2Correct Answer: 240 m2‚Äã‚Äã

questions.question: Two similar triangles, Triangle P and Triangle Q, have perimeters of 30 cm and 45 cm respectively. If the area of Triangle P is 50 cm¬≤, what is the area of Triangle Q?
questions.explanation: ‚ÄãGiven:Perimeter of Triangle P=30 cmPerimeter of Triangle Q=45 cmArea of Triangle P=50 cm2Formula Used:For similar triangles,Area1Area2=(Side1Side2)2Solution:Ratio of corresponding sides=Perimeter of PPerimeter of Q=3045=2350Area of Q=(23)2=49Area of Q=50√ó94=112.5 cm2Area of Triangle Q=112.5 cm2\textbf{Given:} \\[4pt]\text{Perimeter of Triangle } P = 30 \text{ cm} \\[4pt]\text{Perimeter of Triangle } Q = 45 \text{ cm} \\[4pt]\text{Area of Triangle } P = 50 \text{ cm}^2 \\[8pt]\textbf{Formula Used:} \\[4pt]\text{For similar triangles,} \\[2pt]\frac{\text{Area}_1}{\text{Area}_2}=\left(\frac{\text{Side}_1}{\text{Side}_2}\right)^2 \\[8pt]\textbf{Solution:} \\[6pt]\text{Ratio of corresponding sides} \\[2pt]=\frac{\text{Perimeter of } P}{\text{Perimeter of } Q}=\frac{30}{45}=\frac{2}{3} \\[10pt]\frac{50}{\text{Area of } Q}=\left(\frac{2}{3}\right)^2=\frac{4}{9} \\[10pt]\text{Area of } Q=50 \times \frac{9}{4}=112.5 \text{ cm}^2 \\[10pt]\boxed{\text{Area of Triangle } Q = 112.5 \text{ cm}^2}Given:Perimeter of Triangle P=30 cmPerimeter of Triangle Q=45 cmArea of Triangle P=50 cm2Formula Used:For similar triangles,Area2‚ÄãArea1‚Äã‚Äã=(Side2‚ÄãSide1‚Äã‚Äã)2Solution:Ratio of corresponding sides=Perimeter of QPerimeter of P‚Äã=4530‚Äã=32‚ÄãArea of Q50‚Äã=(32‚Äã)2=94‚ÄãArea of Q=50√ó49‚Äã=112.5 cm2Area of Triangle Q=112.5 cm2‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã

questions.question: The following table shows the number of students opting for different sports in a school: Sport Cricket Football Hockey Badminton Basketball Number of students 48 36 24 42 30 If a pie chart is constructed for the above data, what will be the angle of the sector representing Football?
questions.explanation: Given: ‚ÄãStudents: C=48, F=36, H=24, B=42, Bk=30\text{Students: C=48, F=36, H=24, B=42, Bk=30}Students: C=48, F=36, H=24, B=42, Bk=30‚Äã‚Äã Solution: Total students = 48 + 36 + 24 + 42 + 30 = 180. Football students = 36. Sector Angle = ValueTotal√ó360‚àò \frac{\text{Value}}{\text{Total}} \times 360^\circTotalValue‚Äã√ó360‚àò‚Äã‚Äã Angle =36180√ó360‚àò = \frac{36}{180} \times 360^\circ=18036‚Äã√ó360‚àò‚Äã‚Äã ‚Äã=15√ó360‚àò=72‚àò.= \frac{1}{5} \times 360^\circ = 72^\circ.=51‚Äã√ó360‚àò=72‚àò.‚Äã‚Äã So the correct answer is (a).

questions.question: If q=(14.52√ó1011)‚àí(6.4√ó1010)q = (14.52 \times 10^{11}) - (6.4 \times 10^{10})q=(14.52√ó1011)‚àí(6.4√ó1010)‚Äã, then qis expressed in standard form as :
questions.explanation: Given q = (14.52 √ó 10¬π¬π) ‚àí (6.4 √ó 10¬π‚Å∞) Solution First, write both terms with the same power of 10: 6.4 √ó 10¬π‚Å∞ = 0.64 √ó 10¬π¬π Now substitute: q = (14.52 √ó 10¬π¬π) ‚àí (0.64 √ó 10¬π¬π) q = (14.52 ‚àí 0.64) √ó 10¬π¬π q = 13.88 √ó 10¬π¬π Convert to standard form (coefficient between 1 and 10): 13.88 √ó 10¬π¬π = 1.388 √ó 10¬π¬≤ 1.388 √ó 10¬π¬≤ Correct option: A

questions.question: The sum of three numbers P, Q, and R is 4130. If the ratio of P to Q is 3:4 and the ratio of Q to R is 5:6, find the value of Q.
questions.explanation: ‚ÄãGiven:P+Q+R=4130P:Q=3:4Q:R=5:6Formula Used:P:Q:R=ac:bc:bdSolution:P:Q=3:4=15:20Q:R=5:6=20:24P:Q:R=15:20:2415+20+24=59One part=413059=70Q=20√ó70=1400Final Answer:1400\textbf{Given:}\\P + Q + R = 4130\\P:Q = 3:4\\Q:R = 5:6\\[6pt]\\[6pt]\textbf{Formula Used:}\\P:Q:R = ac:bc:bd\\[6pt]\textbf{Solution:}\\P:Q = 3:4 = 15:20\\Q:R = 5:6 = 20:24\\P:Q:R = 15:20:24\\15 + 20 + 24 = 59\\\text{One part} = \frac{4130}{59} = 70\\Q = 20 \times 70 = 1400\\[6pt]\textbf{Final Answer:}\\\boxed{1400}Given:P+Q+R=4130P:Q=3:4Q:R=5:6Formula Used:P:Q:R=ac:bc:bdSolution:P:Q=3:4=15:20Q:R=5:6=20:24P:Q:R=15:20:2415+20+24=59One part=594130‚Äã=70Q=20√ó70=1400Final Answer:1400‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã

questions.question: D is a point on side AB of a triangle ABC such that CD = AD = BD. If ‚àÝ\angle ‚àÝ‚ÄãBAC = 25‚àò25^\circ25‚àò‚Äã, then the value of (3‚àÝ(3 \angle(3‚àÝ‚Äã ABC - ‚àÝ \angle ‚àÝ‚ÄãACB)is :
questions.explanation: Given: Triangle ABC with D on AB such that AD = BD = CD. ‚Äã‚àÝBAC=25‚àò.\angle BAC = 25^\circ.‚àÝBAC=25‚àò.‚Äã‚Äã Concept Used: If the median to a side is half the length of that side, the triangle is right-angled at the vertex opposite to that side. Alternatively, D is the circumcenter lying on the hypotenuse AB. Solution: Analyze angles. Since AD = CD, ‚ñ≥\triangle ‚ñ≥‚ÄãADC is isosceles. ‚Äã‚àÝACD=‚àÝCAD=25‚àò.\angle ACD = \angle CAD = 25^\circ.‚àÝACD=‚àÝCAD=25‚àò.‚Äã‚Äã Since BD = CD, ‚ñ≥BDC\triangle BDC‚ñ≥BDC‚Äã is isosceles. Let ‚àÝCBD=‚àÝBCD=x. \angle CBD = \angle BCD = x.‚àÝCBD=‚àÝBCD=x.‚Äã‚Äã Find x. Sum of angles in \triangle ABC is 180‚àò.180^\circ.180‚àò.‚Äã‚Äã ‚Äã‚àÝA+‚àÝB+‚àÝC=180‚àò25‚àò+x+(25‚àò+x)=180‚àò50‚àò+2x=180‚àò ‚üπ 2x=130‚àò ‚üπ x=65‚àò\angle A + \angle B + \angle C = 180^\circ\\25^\circ + x + (25^\circ + x) = 180^\circ\\50^\circ + 2x = 180^\circ \implies 2x = 130^\circ \implies x = 65^\circ‚àÝA+‚àÝB+‚àÝC=180‚àò25‚àò+x+(25‚àò+x)=180‚àò50‚àò+2x=180‚àò‚üπ2x=130‚àò‚üπx=65‚àò‚Äã‚Äã So, ‚àÝABC=65‚àòand‚àÝACB=25+65=90‚àò.\angle ABC = 65^\circ and \angle ACB = 25+65 = 90^\circ.‚àÝABC=65‚àòand‚àÝACB=25+65=90‚àò.‚Äã‚Äã Calculate the required value. So the correct answer is (b)

questions.question: When a shopkeeper sells item P for ‚Çπ 510, then there is a loss of 15% and when he sells item Q for ‚Çπ 720, then there is a profit of 20%. What is the profit/loss percent, if he sells both items for a total of ‚Çπ 1,320?
questions.explanation: Given: Item P: SP = ‚Çπ 510, Loss = 15% Item Q: SP = ‚Çπ 720, Profit = 20% Combined SP = ‚Çπ 1320 Formula Used: CP =SP√ó100100¬±Profit/Loss%= \frac{SP \times 100}{100 \pm \text{Profit/Loss}\%}=100¬±Profit/Loss%SP√ó100‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã Profit = SP - CP ‚ÄãProfit%=ProfitCP√ó100\text{Profit}\% = \frac{\text{Profit}}{CP} \times 100\\Profit%=CPProfit‚Äã√ó100‚Äã‚Äã Solution: Calculate CP of Item P. ‚ÄãCPP=510√ó100100‚àí15=5100085=600CP_P = \frac{510 \times 100}{100 - 15} = \frac{51000}{85} = 600\\CPP‚Äã=100‚àí15510√ó100‚Äã=8551000‚Äã=600‚Äã‚Äã Calculate CP of Item Q. ‚ÄãCPQ=720√ó100100+20=72000120=600CP_Q = \frac{720 \times 100}{100 + 20} = \frac{72000}{120} = 600\\CPQ‚Äã=100+20720√ó100‚Äã=12072000‚Äã=600‚Äã‚Äã Calculate Total CP and Total SP. Total CP = 600 + 600 = 1200 Total SP = 1320 (Given) Calculate Profit Percent. Profit = 1320 - 1200 = 120\ ‚ÄãProfit%=1201200√ó100=10%\text{Profit}\% = \frac{120}{1200} \times 100 = 10\%\\Profit%=1200120‚Äã√ó100=10%‚Äã‚Äã So the correct answer is (a)

questions.question: Two trains 105 m and 90 m long run at speeds of 45 km/hr and 72 km/hr in opposite directions. Time to cross?
questions.explanation: ‚ÄãGiven: Length of trains: L1=105 m,L2=90 m Speeds: S1=45 km/hr,S2=72 km/hr Direction: Opposite Concept Used: Relative speed in opposite direction is sum of speeds. Formula Used: Time=Total DistanceRelative Speed Solution: Total Distance=L1+L2=105+90=195 m Relative Speed=45+72=117 km/hr Convert speed to m/s: 117√ó518=13√ó52=652=32.5 m/s Calculate Time: T=19532.5 T=6 seconds Final Answer 6 Seconds\textbf{Given:} \\ \space \, \\ \text{Length of trains: } L_1 = 105\text{ m}, L_2 = 90\text{ m} \\ \space \, \\ \text{Speeds: } S_1 = 45\text{ km/hr}, S_2 = 72\text{ km/hr} \\ \space \, \\ \text{Direction: Opposite} \\ \space \, \\ \textbf{Concept Used:} \\ \space \, \\ \text{Relative speed in opposite direction is sum of speeds.} \\ \space \, \\ \textbf{Formula Used:} \\ \space \, \\ \text{Time} = \frac{\text{Total Distance}}{\text{Relative Speed}} \\ \space \, \\ \textbf{Solution:} \\ \space \, \\ \text{Total Distance} = L_1 + L_2 = 105 + 90 = 195\text{ m} \\ \space \, \\ \text{Relative Speed} = 45 + 72 = 117\text{ km/hr} \\ \space \, \\ \text{Convert speed to m/s:} \\ \space \, \\ 117 \times \frac{5}{18} = \frac{13 \times 5}{2} = \frac{65}{2} = 32.5\text{ m/s} \\ \space \, \\ \text{Calculate Time:} \\ \space \, \\ T = \frac{195}{32.5} \\ \space \, \\ T = 6 \text{ seconds} \\ \space \, \\ \textbf{Final Answer} \\ \space \, \\ 6 \text{ Seconds}Given: Length of trains: L1‚Äã=105 m,L2‚Äã=90 m Speeds: S1‚Äã=45 km/hr,S2‚Äã=72 km/hr Direction: Opposite Concept Used: Relative speed in opposite direction is sum of speeds. Formula Used: Time=Relative SpeedTotal Distance‚Äã Solution: Total Distance=L1‚Äã+L2‚Äã=105+90=195 m Relative Speed=45+72=117 km/hr Convert speed to m/s: 117√ó185‚Äã=213√ó5‚Äã=265‚Äã=32.5 m/s Calculate Time: T=32.5195‚Äã T=6 seconds Final Answer 6 Seconds‚Äã‚Äã
